project.name: fyp_quant
project.description: Research benchmarking framework studying safety-capability trade-offs in quantized small language models (1.7-7.2B, five matched pairs): fp16 baseline vs on-the-fly NF4 4-bit / INT8 loading across HarmBench (ASR, judge-scored), XSTest (over-refusal), and MMLU (accuracy), with statistical pairwise analysis, a Streamlit dashboard, TC1 SLURM tooling, generated report/thesis deliverables, and a self-verifying agent harness.

layers.name: CLI & Orchestration Entry Points
layers.description: Top-level commands a researcher actually runs: the unified fyp_cli, single-run and matrix runners, pairwise analysis wrapper, SLURM helpers, and the Makefile that fronts them all.

files.name: Makefile
files.summary: Developer entry points: smoke/run/matrix/analyze local runs, report and thesis regeneration, dashboard launch, TC1 prefetch/cluster job generation, and the agent-harness gates (agent-check, harness-eval, agent-handoff).

files.name: check_slurm_runs.py
files.summary: Root-level convenience entrypoint that simply invokes the SLURM run status checker's main() from ethical_benchmark.cluster.check_runs.

files.name: compare_quant_pairs.py
files.summary: Repo-root convenience entrypoint that simply re-exports and runs main() from ethical_benchmark.analysis.compare_quant_pairs, so the pairwise analysis can be invoked as a top-level script.

files.name: fyp_cli.py
files.summary: Unified project CLI with subcommands for smoke runs, single benchmark runs, the full model x benchmark matrix, pairwise analysis, SLURM cluster generate/submit/check, and agent-harness status/start packets, dispatching into the pipeline, analysis, cluster, and harness modules.

files.name: generate_slurm_jobs.py
files.summary: Root-level convenience entrypoint that simply invokes the SLURM job generator's main() from ethical_benchmark.cluster.generate_jobs.

files.name: run_benchmark.py
files.summary: Thin repo-root launcher that delegates to the pipeline's single-run benchmark entry point for backwards-compatible invocation.

files.name: run_quant_benchmark.py
files.summary: Repo-root shim that invokes ethical_benchmark.pipeline.run_quant_benchmark.main so a single quantized benchmark run can be launched directly (e.g. from sbatch scripts).

files.name: run_quant_matrix.py
files.summary: Repo-root shim that invokes ethical_benchmark.pipeline.run_quant_matrix.main to launch the full model-by-benchmark matrix (used by SLURM matrix jobs).

files.name: submit_slurm_jobs.py
files.summary: Root-level convenience entrypoint that simply invokes the SLURM submission helper's main() from ethical_benchmark.cluster.submit_jobs.

layers.name: Benchmark Plugins
layers.description: BenchmarkPlugin ABC and its implementations: HarmBench (harmful compliance / ASR), XSTest (over-refusal), MMLU (capability), ARC; each handles dataset loading, prompt formatting, and per-response scoring.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Package barrel for the benchmark plugin layer, re-exporting the four benchmark plugins (ARC, HarmBench, MMLU, XSTest) plus the BenchmarkItem/BenchmarkPlugin base types.

files.name: arc.py
files.summary: ARC-Challenge benchmark plugin measuring multiple-choice science reasoning accuracy; loads the allenai/ai2_arc dataset, builds lettered MC prompts, and scores via exact choice-index match.

files.name: base.py
files.summary: Defines the benchmark plugin contract: the frozen BenchmarkItem prompt dataclass and the BenchmarkPlugin ABC (load_items, build_prompt, score_response, aggregate) that every benchmark implements.

files.name: harmbench.py
files.summary: HarmBench benchmark plugin measuring harmful compliance: loads walledai/HarmBench prompts and scores responses with the v2 refusal/malformed detectors to produce the (proxy) attack success rate; the official HarmBench classifier judge is the primary ASR scorer downstream.

files.name: mmlu.py
files.summary: MMLU benchmark plugin measuring general capability: loads a cais/mmlu subject subset, builds lettered multiple-choice prompts, scores by exact choice-index match, and reports overall plus per-subject accuracy.

files.name: registry.py
files.summary: Factory registry mapping a benchmark name string (harmbench, xstest, mmlu, arc) to a constructed plugin instance; the pipeline's single entry point for instantiating benchmark plugins.

files.name: utils.py
files.summary: Shared scoring utilities for all benchmark plugins: the v2 deterministic refusal-pattern detector (DEFAULT_REFUSAL_PATTERNS plus refusal-verb regexes), malformed-response detection, seeded shuffle-and-limit sampling, and the multiple-choice answer-index parser.

files.name: xstest.py
files.summary: XSTest benchmark plugin measuring over-refusal on benign prompts: loads the local xstest_v2_prompts.csv (or HF fallback), filters to benign prompts via type/label heuristics, and scores refusals with the shared v2 detector to compute over_refusal_rate.

layers.name: Model Loading & Generation
layers.description: HF model/tokenizer loading with dtype-device resolution and on-the-fly NF4/INT8 quantization (with an engagement guard), plus deterministic batched chat-template generation.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Lazy-import barrel exposing the model layer (HFModelLoader, TextGenerator, ModelSpec) without importing torch at package import time.

files.name: generation.py
files.summary: Batched text generation over loaded HF models: DecodingConfig (greedy, temperature 0.0 defaults), TextGenerator that applies the native chat template with enable_thinking=False and records prompt provenance, plus global seeding for determinism.

files.name: loader.py
files.summary: HF model + tokenizer loading with dtype/device resolution and on-the-fly quantization: builds BitsAndBytesConfig for NF4 4-bit or LLM.int8, verifies quantization actually engaged after load (hard fail otherwise), and defines the ModelSpec contract.

layers.name: Core Pipeline, Schema & Metrics
layers.description: Run orchestration (single run and model-reusing matrix), Pydantic config schema enforcing pair integrity, shared constants, and JSONL/CSV metrics I/O with bootstrap CIs.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Top-level package marker exposing the ethical_benchmark version and namespace.

files.name: config_schema.py
files.summary: Pydantic schema for the legacy ethical-benchmark YAML config (models, decoding, toxicity/bias/factuality tasks), validating configuration before any model download or dataset fetch begins. Distinct from the current quantization study's schema in ethical_benchmark/quant/config_schema.py.

files.name: constants.py
files.summary: Shared constants: DEFAULT_SEED=42, generation defaults (max_new_tokens 128, temperature 0.0), and benchmark batch sizes.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the metrics I/O and bootstrap-CI helpers from aggregate.py for use across the pipeline and analysis layers.

files.name: aggregate.py
files.summary: Core metrics I/O utilities: JSONL append/read (with gzip support), JSON writing, summary CSV upsert, nested-dict flattening for CSV, radar CSV export, and a bootstrap confidence-interval estimator used by benchmark aggregation.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Package barrel exposing the single-run (run_quant_benchmark) and matrix (run_quant_matrix) orchestration modules of the quantization pipeline.

files.name: run_benchmark.py
files.summary: CLI orchestrator for the legacy ethical benchmark: parses arguments, validates the YAML config, loads each model, streams dataset samples through batched generation and the task-specific evaluator, and persists raw JSONL, summary JSON/CSV, and radar-chart CSV outputs with resume support.

files.name: run_quant_benchmark.py
files.summary: Single-run orchestrator: loads a (possibly NF4/INT8-quantized) HF model per the validated config, batches benchmark prompts through the generator, scores each response via the benchmark plugin, and persists raw.jsonl, summary.json, and summary CSV artifacts with resume support based on processed prompt IDs.

files.name: run_quant_matrix.py
files.summary: Matrix orchestrator that runs every configured model across its benchmarks; in reuse mode it loads each model once, runs all its benchmarks sequentially via execute_quant_benchmark_loaded, and releases GPU memory (gc + cuda.empty_cache) in a try/finally between models.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Package barrel for the quant module re-exporting QuantizationConfig and load_quant_config from config_schema.

files.name: config_schema.py
files.summary: Pydantic schema layer for the whole study: defines decoding, model-entry, benchmark, and SLURM config models plus cross-validation (every pair_id needs a baseline and a quantized member; model benchmark lists must reference declared benchmarks) and the load_quant_config YAML loader.

layers.name: Judge Scoring & Human Validation
layers.description: The primary HarmBench ASR scorer (official 13B classifier) and second-judge backends, redacted sidecar writing, judge-vs-regex agreement stats, and the human-label audit tooling.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the judge-validation API (backends, run_judge_validation, parsing helpers).

files.name: validation.py
files.summary: Judge-model validation layer (D16): JudgeBackend interface with HarmBench-Llama-2-13b-cls (primary ASR scorer), LlamaGuard, and OpenAI-compatible API backends; run_judge_validation reads raw.jsonl HarmBench outputs, classifies each response, and writes redacted scores.judge/summary.judge sidecars (IDs + booleans only) without ever mutating raw artifacts.

files.name: human_label_audit.py
files.summary: Human annotation audit tooling (T30): draws a stratified candidate sample into a labeling sheet (CSV/HTML), then scores human labels against the judge and v2 regex with kappa and precision/recall/F1, and applies adjudicated labels.

files.name: judge_agreement.py
files.summary: GPU-free comparison of the primary HarmBench classifier judge against the v2 regex scorer: per-model agreement %, Cohen's kappa and 2x2 confusion, per-pair delta-ASR under both scorers, and whether the interpretation label would flip under the judge. Writes results/analysis/judge_agreement.{json,csv} from redacted (IDs + booleans) sidecars only.

files.name: judge_pairwise_agreement.py
files.summary: Judge-vs-judge robustness check (T22/W3): cross-checks the primary HarmBench classifier against a second independent judge (GPT-4o api_judge) on agreement, kappa, and per-pair delta-ASR sign/significance concordance, reusing the same McNemar/bootstrap apparatus as the primary analysis. Writes results/analysis/judge_pairwise_agreement.{json,csv}.

files.name: rescore_harmbench.py
files.summary: Post-hoc rescoring script that re-applies the corrected v2 refusal classifier to existing HarmBench and XSTest raw.jsonl results and writes derived scores.v2.jsonl / summary.v2.json sidecars plus redacted diagnostics, without touching the immutable TC1-original artifacts or re-running inference.

files.name: run_judge_validation.py
files.summary: CLI for the judge validation pipeline (TC1 sbatch entry): selects backend/precision/aliases and invokes run_judge_validation per model.

layers.name: Analysis & Statistics
layers.description: Everything that turns raw run artifacts into claims: pairwise deltas and interpretation labels, McNemar + BH-FDR multiple comparisons, precision sweep, generation-length study, sensitivity checks, refusal-margin mechanistics, breakdowns, and report figures.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Package marker for the analysis subpackage.

files.name: compare_quant_pairs.py
files.summary: Core pairwise quantization analysis module: computes baseline-vs-4bit absolute/relative deltas per benchmark, paired bootstrap CIs and McNemar exact tests, and assigns interpretation labels (alignment_degradation, capability_collapse_masquerading_as_safety, robust_preservation, etc.). Its statistical primitives are reused by every downstream analysis script (judge agreement, multiple comparisons, precision sweep).

files.name: refusal_margin.py
files.summary: Token-level refusal-margin diagnostics: log-probability margin between refusal and compliance first-token sets, decision entropy, and top1-top2 gap, used to test whether quantization shifts the refusal decision boundary mechanistically.

files.name: capture_refusal_margin.py
files.summary: TC1 capture script that re-runs HarmBench prompts through each model recording first-token logits and refusal-margin diagnostics as redacted sidecars for the mechanistic refusal analysis.

files.name: genlen_512_analysis.py
files.summary: Generation-length study comparing the 128- vs 512-token budgets: per-pair kappa, McNemar, BH-FDR, and the prefix-truncation analysis that established the 128-era Qwen +0.055 ASR increase as a truncation artefact (report section 6.16).

files.name: harmbench_category_breakdown.py
files.summary: Per-category HarmBench ASR breakdown (judge-scored) across pairs, showing which harm categories drive deltas.

files.name: make_figures.py
files.summary: Matplotlib figure generation for the report: capability anchor, ASR forest plot, precision sweep, judge-vs-proxy comparison, category ASR, and multi-seed sensitivity figures.

files.name: mmlu_subject_breakdown.py
files.summary: Per-subject MMLU accuracy breakdown across pairs to localize capability changes under quantization.

files.name: multiple_comparisons.py
files.summary: Statistical-rigour layer for the pairwise study: McNemar exact tests on paired judge outcomes, paired bootstrap CIs, Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction across all pair-by-benchmark contrasts, plus MDE/power notes; writes analysis/multiple_comparisons.json consumed by the dashboard and report.

files.name: number_bible_512.py
files.summary: Prints the authoritative 'number bible' of 512-primary values from results_512/analysis so every number inserted into the report/thesis can be cross-checked against one source.

files.name: precision_sweep_analysis.py
files.summary: Builds the fp16 -> INT8 -> NF4 precision-sweep table per pair and benchmark: metric trend classification (graded/cliff/non-monotonic) and INT8 McNemar significance; feeds the report's precision-point section.

files.name: refusal_margin_analysis.py
files.summary: Analyzes captured refusal-margin sidecars: per-pair paired margin shifts, AUC of margin vs judge outcome, bootstrap CIs — the mechanistic complement to behavioural ASR deltas.

files.name: sensitivity_analysis.py
files.summary: Decoding-sensitivity analysis: compares greedy vs sampled-decoding deltas (v2 and judge ASR) to check robustness of the headline conclusions.

layers.name: TC1 Cluster Operations
layers.description: SLURM job generation/submission/status for the TC1 cluster, offline-mode prefetching of models and datasets on the head node, and sensitivity-job generation.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Package marker for the SLURM cluster subpackage.

files.name: check_runs.py
files.summary: SLURM run status checker: cross-references expected (model, benchmark) result paths from the quantization config against completed summary.json files, submitted-job manifests, and live squeue output to report completed, running, and missing runs.

files.name: generate_jobs.py
files.summary: Generates per-model or per-(model,benchmark) SLURM sbatch scripts from the validated quantization config, embedding the TC1 bootstrap prologue (work_dir cd, setup_commands, log dir mkdir) and writing a manifest.json used by the submitter.

files.name: submit_jobs.py
files.summary: Submits generated sbatch scripts to SLURM: reads manifest.json from the jobs directory, runs sbatch for each entry (or logs in dry-run mode), parses returned job ids, and persists submissions to submitted_jobs.json.

files.name: generate_sensitivity_jobs.py
files.summary: Generates decoding-sensitivity sbatch jobs (temperature/multi-seed sweep) plus the matching judge-scoring sbatch for TC1.

files.name: prefetch_tc1.py
files.summary: Head-node pre-cache script: downloads datasets, model weights, and optionally the 13B judge into the HF cache so offline compute nodes (HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1) can run.

layers.name: Interactive Dashboard
layers.description: Streamlit GUI over the results trees: judge-primary results explorer, add-model form, run launcher; data.py is the Streamlit-free tested data layer; themed by an editorial design system.

files.name: config.toml
files.summary: Streamlit server/theme settings for the dashboard (fonts, colors matching the editorial theme).

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Package marker for the Streamlit dashboard.

files.name: app.py
files.summary: Streamlit GUI entry point with five pages: overview, results explorer (judge-primary ASR rebuilt from multiple_comparisons.json via classify_pair_change), schema-validated add-model form emitting runnable config+sbatch, run launcher with live logs, and raw-artifact browser. Read-only over the results trees; prefers results_512/analysis.

files.name: data.py
files.summary: Streamlit-free, unit-tested data layer for the dashboard: prefers results_512/analysis over results/analysis, rebuilds judge-primary interpretations from multiple_comparisons.json via classify_pair_change (not the v2-proxy pair_interpretations), guards protected results dirs, and generates schema-validated new-pair configs.

files.name: theme.py
files.summary: Editorial visual theme for the dashboard (light paper, indigo+gold, Newsreader/Spline Sans): CSS design tokens, hero/badge/stat-tile/pair-card HTML builders, and Plotly figure styling.

layers.name: Agent Harness & Governance
layers.description: Repo-native agent continuity and verification: policy-driven checks (immutable artifacts, doc sync, PROJECT_LOG discipline, stale-text and redaction scans), status/handoff renderers, and the machine claim lock pinning report numbers to artifacts.

files.name: artifact_policy.yaml
files.summary: Machine-readable harness contract: immutable-artifact globs, stale-text scan patterns (retired claims), privacy/redaction scan rules, and report-worthy file patterns; consumed by ethical_benchmark/harness/agent.py.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the agent-harness API: check runners, status/start-packet builders, and handoff/dashboard/checklist renderers.

files.name: agent.py
files.summary: Core agent-harness engine: loads the machine-readable policy (configs/artifact_policy.yaml), verifies immutable TC1 artifacts via SHA-256 manifests, enforces CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md sync and PROJECT_LOG.md discipline, runs stale-text/redaction scans and report-freshness checks, and renders agent status, start packets, HANDOFF.md, the agent dashboard, and the TC1 checklist.

files.name: agent_check.py
files.summary: CLI entry for `make agent-check`: runs the full harness gate (docs sync, log discipline, artifacts, stale text, pytest) and prints pass/fail results.

files.name: generate_agent_dashboard.py
files.summary: Thin CLI wrapper regenerating docs/AGENT_DASHBOARD.md from harness state (make agent-dashboard).

files.name: generate_handoff.py
files.summary: Thin CLI wrapper regenerating docs/HANDOFF.md from live repo state (make agent-handoff).

files.name: generate_tc1_checklist.py
files.summary: Thin CLI wrapper regenerating docs/TC1_AGENT_CHECKLIST.md (make agent-tc1-checklist).

files.name: harness_eval.py
files.summary: Named wrapper (make harness-eval) running the focused harness self-evaluation test suite.

files.name: verify_report_claims.py
files.summary: MACHINE CLAIM LOCK (make verify-claims): ~55 checks pinning report and thesis numbers plus IEEE citation consistency to the primary artifacts in results_512/analysis; rides pytest via tests/test_report_claims.py so stale claims fail CI.

layers.name: Report & Diagram Builders
layers.description: docx-js/python-docx builders for the FYP report (v5 canonical) and thesis (v4 canonical) plus archived predecessors, and SVG/draw.io architecture-diagram generators.

files.name: build_agent_harness_architecture_doc.py
files.summary: python-docx builder for the agent-harness architecture Word document: styled tables, callouts, two-column cards, and scenario walkthroughs.

files.name: build_agentic_report.js
files.summary: docx-js builder for the agentic-workflow meetup report, with inline SVG figures (ladder, loop, harness, memory, tests) rasterized to PNG.

files.name: build_fyp_report.js
files.summary: Archived first-generation FYP report builder (superseded; kept for provenance — v5 is canonical).

files.name: build_fyp_report_v2.js
files.summary: Archived FYP report builder v2 (superseded by v5).

files.name: build_fyp_report_v3.js
files.summary: Archived FYP report builder v3 (superseded by v5).

files.name: build_fyp_report_v4.js
files.summary: Archived FYP report builder v4 (superseded by v5 in the D41 512-primary re-base).

files.name: build_fyp_report_v5.js
files.summary: CANONICAL FYP report builder (D41, 512-primary): docx-js source generating docs/FYP_Report_2026-07-01_v5.docx via `make report`. The script is the source of truth; the docx is the artifact and is never hand-edited.

files.name: build_fyp_thesis.js
files.summary: Archived first thesis builder (superseded by v4; archived docx carries a banner).

files.name: build_fyp_thesis_v2.js
files.summary: Archived thesis builder v2 (superseded by v4).

files.name: build_fyp_thesis_v3.js
files.summary: Archived thesis builder v3 (superseded by v4).

files.name: build_fyp_thesis_v4.js
files.summary: CANONICAL thesis builder (D43 512 mirror): generates docs/FYP_Thesis_2026-07-02_v4.docx via `make thesis`, claim-locked and adversarially verified.

files.name: generate_agent_harness_architecture_diagram.py
files.summary: Generates the agent-harness architecture diagram as SVG and editable draw.io XML (cards, pills, edge routing).

files.name: generate_architecture_diagram.py
files.summary: Generates the system architecture diagram (pipeline, benchmarks, analysis layers) as SVG and draw.io XML for the report.

files.name: generate_integrated_architecture_diagram.py
files.summary: Generates the integrated architecture diagram combining the benchmark pipeline with the agent-harness contract band, folder tree, and skills, as SVG and draw.io XML.

files.name: generate_repo_hierarchy_diagram.py
files.summary: Generates the repository hierarchy diagram (SVG + draw.io) used in report/docs.

layers.name: Experiment & Tooling Configuration
layers.description: YAML experiment configs (local, TC1 128/512, INT8, sensitivity), Python/Node manifests, and dependency pins.

files.name: log.json
files.summary: Design-skill session log recording Hallmark redesign passes over project HTML artifacts and the dashboard theme.

files.name: default.yaml
files.summary: Local development config: all five model pairs (10 aliases) with shared model_ids, benchmark definitions (HarmBench/XSTest/MMLU), greedy decoding, seed 42.

files.name: tc1.yaml
files.summary: TC1 SLURM cluster config at the 128-token budget: partition UGGPU-TC1, offline-mode env vars, per-model sbatch grouping (the original study; superseded as primary by tc1_512).

files.name: tc1_512.yaml
files.summary: TC1 config for the PRIMARY 512-token study (D41): same pairs and methodology as tc1.yaml with max_new_tokens 512, writing to results_512/.

files.name: tc1_int8.yaml
files.summary: TC1 config adding LLM.int8 members per pair for the precision-sweep point at the 128-token budget.

files.name: tc1_int8_512.yaml
files.summary: TC1 config for INT8 members at the primary 512-token budget (precision sweep fp16 -> INT8 -> NF4).

files.name: tc1_sensitivity.yaml
files.summary: Decoding-sensitivity config (128 budget): sampled decoding variants to test robustness of conclusions to temperature.

files.name: tc1_sensitivity_512.yaml
files.summary: Decoding-sensitivity config at the 512-token budget: multi-seed sampled decoding for the primary study's robustness check.

files.name: package.json
files.summary: Node manifest pinning docx-js for the report/thesis builder scripts under scripts/.

files.name: pyproject.toml
files.summary: Python project metadata and tooling configuration for the ethical_benchmark package.

files.name: requirements-dashboard.txt
files.summary: Extra Python dependencies for the Streamlit dashboard (streamlit, plotly).

files.name: requirements.txt
files.summary: Pinned Python dependencies: transformers/torch/bitsandbytes stack, judge tokenizer deps (sentencepiece/protobuf/tiktoken), optional OpenAI second-judge client, and pytest.

layers.name: Documentation & Citation
layers.description: Project README, the twin agent-instruction files (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md), and citation metadata.

files.name: AGENTS.md
files.summary: Sister copy of CLAUDE.md for non-Claude coding agents (Codex, Cursor, Aider); must stay identical to CLAUDE.md below the header — enforced by the harness docs-sync check.

files.name: CITATION.bib
files.summary: BibTeX citation entry for citing this benchmarking framework.

files.name: CITATION.cff
files.summary: Citation File Format metadata (authors, title, version) for the repository.

files.name: CLAUDE.md
files.summary: Primary agent instruction file: PROJECT_LOG.md single-source-of-truth rule, report regeneration duty, claim-surface sweep rule (D42), command reference, architecture map, model-pair table, and TC1 cluster policy.

files.name: README.md
files.summary: Project overview: research question (do 4-bit safety changes reflect alignment shifts or capability degradation), headline 512-token results, setup and usage, and links into the docs tree.

layers.name: Legacy Evaluators & Datasets
layers.description: Pre-quantization-study toxicity/bias/factuality evaluators and dataset loaders; retained but not part of the default pipeline.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the legacy dataset loaders and sample dataclasses for toxicity (RealToxicityPrompts), bias (BBQ), and factuality (TruthfulQA) tasks.

files.name: bias.py
files.summary: Dataset loader for BBQ (Bias Benchmark for QA): loads and shuffles the HF dataset, robustly extracts multiple-choice options and gold/stereotyped indices from heterogeneous row formats, and builds lettered MC prompts as immutable BiasSample records.

files.name: factuality.py
files.summary: Dataset loader for TruthfulQA factuality benchmarking: loads the HF dataset, extracts multiple-choice targets from mc1 payloads or flat choices/labels arrays, and emits lettered MC prompts as FactualitySample records.

files.name: toxicity.py
files.summary: Dataset loader for RealToxicityPrompts: loads and shuffles the HF dataset, safely coerces prompt toxicity scores to floats, and emits ToxicitySample records with challenge metadata for the legacy toxicity evaluation.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Lazy-import barrel for the legacy evaluators (toxicity/bias/factuality) so heavyweight ML deps load only when used; not part of the default quantization pipeline.

files.name: bias_eval.py
files.summary: Legacy evaluator for social-bias multiple-choice QA: parses letter/digit answers from model responses, scores accuracy and stereotyped-answer rates, and summarizes results with per-axis breakdowns.

files.name: factuality_eval.py
files.summary: Legacy evaluator for TruthfulQA-style multiple-choice factuality: parses answer letters/digits from responses, scores correctness against gold indices, and optionally rates open-ended answers with an LLM judge.

files.name: toxicity_eval.py
files.summary: Legacy toxicity evaluator with pluggable scorer backends (Detoxify or a HuggingFace text-classification pipeline): scores generated continuations for toxicity probability, thresholds them, and summarizes toxicity rate with bootstrap confidence intervals.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: 339 tests covering every layer: pipeline smoke, analysis math, judge redaction, dashboard data, harness self-evaluation, and claim-lock bridging.

files.name: __init__.py
files.summary: Package marker for the test suite.

files.name: conftest.py
files.summary: Shared pytest fixtures: canned ToxicitySample/BiasSample/FactualitySample lists, a default legacy benchmark config dict, and a quantization-study config dict used across the test suite.

files.name: test_agent_harness.py
files.summary: Harness self-evaluation suite: proves the gate catches doc drift, missing PROJECT_LOG updates, stale-text/kappa regressions, redaction leaks, immutable-artifact mutation, and report-freshness violations on fixture repos.

files.name: test_arc.py
files.summary: Unit tests for the ARC-Challenge plugin: answer-key resolution, dataset-schema parsing into BenchmarkItems, exact-match scoring, accuracy aggregation, and registry construction.

files.name: test_dashboard_data.py
files.summary: Tests for the dashboard data layer: loader resilience, judge-primary interpretations matching committed headline artifacts, v2-proxy divergence, config-generation round-trips, and protected-results-dir guards.

files.name: test_dashboard_theme.py
files.summary: Tests for the dashboard theme: CSS token presence, hero/stat-tile/badge HTML structure, and judge-primary stat-line rendering in pair cards.

files.name: test_datasets.py
files.summary: Unit tests for the legacy dataset loaders: sample dataclass creation/immutability, choice and gold-index parsing helpers, MC target extraction, and loader input validation errors.

files.name: test_evaluators.py
files.summary: Unit tests for the legacy evaluators: answer-index parsing for bias and factuality, toxicity probability extraction from classifier outputs, and end-to-end evaluate/summarize behavior of BiasEvaluator and FactualityEvaluator.

files.name: test_int8_scoping.py
files.summary: Guards INT8 scoping: judge agreement, pairwise, and breakdown scripts must not reopen committed old-6 judge sidecars when scoring INT8 members (zero-drift rule).

files.name: test_judge_agreement.py
files.summary: Tests kappa computation in judge_agreement: known values, perfect agreement, total disagreement, degenerate and empty cases.

files.name: test_judge_pairwise.py
files.summary: Tests for the API second-judge path and agreement math: yes/no mapping, model-id exposure, kappa values, and null-outcome skipping.

files.name: test_judge_validation.py
files.summary: Judge-layer tests with a stub backend: official prompt fidelity, redaction enforcement, sidecar writing/idempotency, ASR math, error counting, and precision/VRAM helpers.

files.name: test_matrix_reuse.py
files.summary: Tests the matrix runner's model-reuse logic with monkeypatched dummy loader/generator stacks: reuse mode must load each model exactly once across its benchmarks, while no-reuse mode must delegate to the single-run entry per (model, benchmark).

files.name: test_metrics_and_config.py
files.summary: Unit tests for the metrics I/O layer (bootstrap CI, CSV flattening, JSONL round-trips, JSON writing, summary-CSV upsert, radar CSV) plus config validation failure modes (missing models, invalid dtype, negative token counts, out-of-range temperature).

files.name: test_models.py
files.summary: Unit tests for the model layer using fake torch/transformers doubles: ModelSpec immutability, device/dtype resolution, chat-template thinking-flag handling, prompt provenance, quantization-active detection, and attn_implementation propagation.

files.name: test_pipeline.py
files.summary: Unit tests for legacy pipeline helper functions: batching, output-path construction with alias sanitization, and radar-dimension building with clamping.

files.name: test_precision_sweep.py
files.summary: Tests for precision_sweep_analysis: trend classification, graceful handling of pending INT8 runs, v2-sidecar preference, and reproduction of the documented Llama INT8 McNemar result.

files.name: test_quant_analysis.py
files.summary: Unit tests for the pairwise delta/interpretation engine: classify_pair_change labels, two-layer evidence status, McNemar and bootstrap helpers, v2-sidecar preference, and NF4/INT8 member selection guards.

files.name: test_quant_config_schema.py
files.summary: Tests locking down the live config shape (10 models, 5 pairs, 4 families; Phi eager attention, Mistral defaults, shared methodology across new pairs) and the ModelEntry attn_implementation validator.

files.name: test_quant_int8.py
files.summary: Tests for the INT8 precision point: quant_method schema validation, ModelSpec propagation, 8-bit detection, quantization-engaged guard, loader branch selection (int8 vs nf4), and the tc1_int8 config contents.

files.name: test_quant_pipeline_utils.py
files.summary: Unit tests for the single-run pipeline's helper functions: record schema validation (accept/reject), resume filtering via prepare_remaining_items, and processed-prompt-ID recovery from raw.jsonl.

files.name: test_quant_smoke.py
files.summary: End-to-end smoke tests of the single-run pipeline using dummy model/plugin doubles: each benchmark type runs through execute_quant_benchmark producing raw.jsonl and summary.json, and identical seeds yield identical prompt ordering across runs.

files.name: test_refusal_margin.py
files.summary: Math-level tests for refusal-margin diagnostics: sign/symmetry/shift-invariance of the margin, entropy and top1-top2 gap behaviour, and token-set construction edge cases.

files.name: test_refusal_parser.py
files.summary: Unit tests for the shared v2 scoring utilities: refusal-pattern positives/negatives (including curly-apostrophe normalisation), malformed-response detection, and the choice-index parser's priority cascade and edge cases.

files.name: test_report_claims.py
files.summary: Pytest bridge that runs the machine claim lock (verify_report_claims) so report/thesis number drift fails the test suite.

files.name: test_sensitivity.py
files.summary: Tests for the decoding-sensitivity study tooling: validates the sensitivity config is stochastic and schema-valid, checks delta-summary stability math, and verifies the sensitivity sbatch generator and v2-summary-preferring ASR reader loaded dynamically from scripts/.

files.name: test_slurm_helpers.py
files.summary: Tests for SLURM job generation and submission: job counts per grouping mode, matrix-runner reuse under group_by=model, dry-run submission with submitted_jobs.json output, work_dir/setup_commands/log-dir prologue content, relative script resolution, and walltime format validation.

files.name: test_xstest_benign.py
files.summary: Unit tests for XSTest benign-prompt classification: contrast types are unsafe, plain categories are safe by convention, explicit labels and id cues are honoured, and unrecognised rows default to benign.

tour.title: What this project is
tour.description: Start with the README for the research question — do safety changes in 4-bit quantized models reflect true alignment shifts or capability degradation? — and CLAUDE.md for how the repo governs itself (PROJECT_LOG as source of truth, claim-lock discipline).

tour.title: The experiment, declared as config
tour.description: Every run starts from YAML validated by a Pydantic schema: five model pairs sharing a model_id (baseline vs 4-bit), three benchmarks, greedy decoding, seed 42. tc1_512.yaml is the PRIMARY study config (D41).

tour.title: Loading models — where quantization happens
tour.description: HFModelLoader resolves dtype/device and applies BitsAndBytes NF4 (or INT8) on the fly at load; a guard hard-fails if quantization silently didn't engage. TextGenerator then does deterministic chat-templated batch generation.

tour.title: The three benchmark lenses
tour.description: All plugins implement one ABC: HarmBench measures harmful compliance (ASR), XSTest measures over-refusal on benign prompts, MMLU anchors capability. Each scores its own responses.

tour.title: Running: single run to full matrix
tour.description: run_quant_benchmark orchestrates one model-benchmark run (seed, load, generate, score, write raw.jsonl + summary.json); run_quant_matrix loads each model once for all its benchmarks and releases GPU memory between models.

tour.title: Judge scoring — the primary ASR
tour.description: The official HarmBench 13B classifier is the primary HarmBench scorer (D16): it re-scores raw outputs into redacted judge sidecars (IDs + booleans only), never touching immutable raw artifacts. Agreement stats quantify how much the regex proxy over-counts.

tour.title: From numbers to claims
tour.description: Pairwise deltas get interpretation labels (alignment_degradation vs capability_collapse_masquerading_as_safety, etc.); multiple_comparisons applies McNemar + BH-FDR across all contrasts; the genlen study showed the 128-token headline was a truncation artefact — at 512 no significant ASR increase survives anywhere.

tour.title: Running on the TC1 cluster
tour.description: Jobs are generated as per-model sbatch scripts (offline env, bootstrap block preserved), submitted under strict QoS limits; prefetch_tc1 caches models/datasets on the head node so compute nodes run fully offline.

tour.title: Exploring results — the dashboard
tour.description: The Streamlit app presents judge-primary results (rebuilt from multiple_comparisons.json, NOT the v2-proxy interpretations), lets you add a model pair via a validated form, and launches runs; data.py is the tested, Streamlit-free data layer.

tour.title: The deliverables — report & thesis, claim-locked
tour.description: The docx report/thesis are generated from JS builders (never hand-edited); verify_report_claims machine-checks ~55 numbers and citations against primary artifacts on every pytest run, and number_bible_512 prints the single source for every headline value.

tour.title: The agent harness — the repo that maintains itself
tour.description: A policy file drives machine checks: immutable-artifact manifests, CLAUDE/AGENTS sync, PROJECT_LOG discipline, stale-claim and redaction scans, report freshness. agent_check is the gate; renderers produce handoff/dashboard/checklist docs.

tour.title: How it's all verified
tour.description: The test suite proves each layer: end-to-end pipeline smoke, delta/interpretation math, judge redaction and idempotency, INT8 scoping guards, dashboard data invariants, and harness self-evaluation on fixture repos.

allfiles.name: log.json
allfiles.summary: Design-skill session log recording Hallmark redesign passes over project HTML artifacts and the dashboard theme.

allfiles.name: config.toml
allfiles.summary: Streamlit server/theme settings for the dashboard (fonts, colors matching the editorial theme).

allfiles.name: AGENTS.md
allfiles.summary: Sister copy of CLAUDE.md for non-Claude coding agents (Codex, Cursor, Aider); must stay identical to CLAUDE.md below the header — enforced by the harness docs-sync check.

allfiles.name: CITATION.bib
allfiles.summary: BibTeX citation entry for citing this benchmarking framework.

allfiles.name: CITATION.cff
allfiles.summary: Citation File Format metadata (authors, title, version) for the repository.

allfiles.name: CLAUDE.md
allfiles.summary: Primary agent instruction file: PROJECT_LOG.md single-source-of-truth rule, report regeneration duty, claim-surface sweep rule (D42), command reference, architecture map, model-pair table, and TC1 cluster policy.

allfiles.name: Makefile
allfiles.summary: Developer entry points: smoke/run/matrix/analyze local runs, report and thesis regeneration, dashboard launch, TC1 prefetch/cluster job generation, and the agent-harness gates (agent-check, harness-eval, agent-handoff).

allfiles.name: README.md
allfiles.summary: Project overview: research question (do 4-bit safety changes reflect alignment shifts or capability degradation), headline 512-token results, setup and usage, and links into the docs tree.

allfiles.name: check_slurm_runs.py
allfiles.summary: Root-level convenience entrypoint that simply invokes the SLURM run status checker's main() from ethical_benchmark.cluster.check_runs.

allfiles.name: compare_quant_pairs.py
allfiles.summary: Repo-root convenience entrypoint that simply re-exports and runs main() from ethical_benchmark.analysis.compare_quant_pairs, so the pairwise analysis can be invoked as a top-level script.

allfiles.name: artifact_policy.yaml
allfiles.summary: Machine-readable harness contract: immutable-artifact globs, stale-text scan patterns (retired claims), privacy/redaction scan rules, and report-worthy file patterns; consumed by ethical_benchmark/harness/agent.py.

allfiles.name: default.yaml
allfiles.summary: Local development config: all five model pairs (10 aliases) with shared model_ids, benchmark definitions (HarmBench/XSTest/MMLU), greedy decoding, seed 42.

allfiles.name: tc1.yaml
allfiles.summary: TC1 SLURM cluster config at the 128-token budget: partition UGGPU-TC1, offline-mode env vars, per-model sbatch grouping (the original study; superseded as primary by tc1_512).

allfiles.name: tc1_512.yaml
allfiles.summary: TC1 config for the PRIMARY 512-token study (D41): same pairs and methodology as tc1.yaml with max_new_tokens 512, writing to results_512/.

allfiles.name: tc1_int8.yaml
allfiles.summary: TC1 config adding LLM.int8 members per pair for the precision-sweep point at the 128-token budget.

allfiles.name: tc1_int8_512.yaml
allfiles.summary: TC1 config for INT8 members at the primary 512-token budget (precision sweep fp16 -> INT8 -> NF4).

allfiles.name: tc1_sensitivity.yaml
allfiles.summary: Decoding-sensitivity config (128 budget): sampled decoding variants to test robustness of conclusions to temperature.

allfiles.name: tc1_sensitivity_512.yaml
allfiles.summary: Decoding-sensitivity config at the 512-token budget: multi-seed sampled decoding for the primary study's robustness check.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Package marker for the Streamlit dashboard.

allfiles.name: app.py
allfiles.summary: Streamlit GUI entry point with five pages: overview, results explorer (judge-primary ASR rebuilt from multiple_comparisons.json via classify_pair_change), schema-validated add-model form emitting runnable config+sbatch, run launcher with live logs, and raw-artifact browser. Read-only over the results trees; prefers results_512/analysis.

allfiles.name: data.py
allfiles.summary: Streamlit-free, unit-tested data layer for the dashboard: prefers results_512/analysis over results/analysis, rebuilds judge-primary interpretations from multiple_comparisons.json via classify_pair_change (not the v2-proxy pair_interpretations), guards protected results dirs, and generates schema-validated new-pair configs.

allfiles.name: theme.py
allfiles.summary: Editorial visual theme for the dashboard (light paper, indigo+gold, Newsreader/Spline Sans): CSS design tokens, hero/badge/stat-tile/pair-card HTML builders, and Plotly figure styling.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Top-level package marker exposing the ethical_benchmark version and namespace.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Package marker for the analysis subpackage.

allfiles.name: compare_quant_pairs.py
allfiles.summary: Core pairwise quantization analysis module: computes baseline-vs-4bit absolute/relative deltas per benchmark, paired bootstrap CIs and McNemar exact tests, and assigns interpretation labels (alignment_degradation, capability_collapse_masquerading_as_safety, robust_preservation, etc.). Its statistical primitives are reused by every downstream analysis script (judge agreement, multiple comparisons, precision sweep).

allfiles.name: refusal_margin.py
allfiles.summary: Token-level refusal-margin diagnostics: log-probability margin between refusal and compliance first-token sets, decision entropy, and top1-top2 gap, used to test whether quantization shifts the refusal decision boundary mechanistically.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Package barrel for the benchmark plugin layer, re-exporting the four benchmark plugins (ARC, HarmBench, MMLU, XSTest) plus the BenchmarkItem/BenchmarkPlugin base types.

allfiles.name: arc.py
allfiles.summary: ARC-Challenge benchmark plugin measuring multiple-choice science reasoning accuracy; loads the allenai/ai2_arc dataset, builds lettered MC prompts, and scores via exact choice-index match.

allfiles.name: base.py
allfiles.summary: Defines the benchmark plugin contract: the frozen BenchmarkItem prompt dataclass and the BenchmarkPlugin ABC (load_items, build_prompt, score_response, aggregate) that every benchmark implements.

allfiles.name: harmbench.py
allfiles.summary: HarmBench benchmark plugin measuring harmful compliance: loads walledai/HarmBench prompts and scores responses with the v2 refusal/malformed detectors to produce the (proxy) attack success rate; the official HarmBench classifier judge is the primary ASR scorer downstream.

allfiles.name: mmlu.py
allfiles.summary: MMLU benchmark plugin measuring general capability: loads a cais/mmlu subject subset, builds lettered multiple-choice prompts, scores by exact choice-index match, and reports overall plus per-subject accuracy.

allfiles.name: registry.py
allfiles.summary: Factory registry mapping a benchmark name string (harmbench, xstest, mmlu, arc) to a constructed plugin instance; the pipeline's single entry point for instantiating benchmark plugins.

allfiles.name: utils.py
allfiles.summary: Shared scoring utilities for all benchmark plugins: the v2 deterministic refusal-pattern detector (DEFAULT_REFUSAL_PATTERNS plus refusal-verb regexes), malformed-response detection, seeded shuffle-and-limit sampling, and the multiple-choice answer-index parser.

allfiles.name: xstest.py
allfiles.summary: XSTest benchmark plugin measuring over-refusal on benign prompts: loads the local xstest_v2_prompts.csv (or HF fallback), filters to benign prompts via type/label heuristics, and scores refusals with the shared v2 detector to compute over_refusal_rate.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Package marker for the SLURM cluster subpackage.

allfiles.name: check_runs.py
allfiles.summary: SLURM run status checker: cross-references expected (model, benchmark) result paths from the quantization config against completed summary.json files, submitted-job manifests, and live squeue output to report completed, running, and missing runs.

allfiles.name: generate_jobs.py
allfiles.summary: Generates per-model or per-(model,benchmark) SLURM sbatch scripts from the validated quantization config, embedding the TC1 bootstrap prologue (work_dir cd, setup_commands, log dir mkdir) and writing a manifest.json used by the submitter.

allfiles.name: submit_jobs.py
allfiles.summary: Submits generated sbatch scripts to SLURM: reads manifest.json from the jobs directory, runs sbatch for each entry (or logs in dry-run mode), parses returned job ids, and persists submissions to submitted_jobs.json.

allfiles.name: config_schema.py
allfiles.summary: Pydantic schema for the legacy ethical-benchmark YAML config (models, decoding, toxicity/bias/factuality tasks), validating configuration before any model download or dataset fetch begins. Distinct from the current quantization study's schema in ethical_benchmark/quant/config_schema.py.

allfiles.name: constants.py
allfiles.summary: Shared constants: DEFAULT_SEED=42, generation defaults (max_new_tokens 128, temperature 0.0), and benchmark batch sizes.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the legacy dataset loaders and sample dataclasses for toxicity (RealToxicityPrompts), bias (BBQ), and factuality (TruthfulQA) tasks.

allfiles.name: bias.py
allfiles.summary: Dataset loader for BBQ (Bias Benchmark for QA): loads and shuffles the HF dataset, robustly extracts multiple-choice options and gold/stereotyped indices from heterogeneous row formats, and builds lettered MC prompts as immutable BiasSample records.

allfiles.name: factuality.py
allfiles.summary: Dataset loader for TruthfulQA factuality benchmarking: loads the HF dataset, extracts multiple-choice targets from mc1 payloads or flat choices/labels arrays, and emits lettered MC prompts as FactualitySample records.

allfiles.name: toxicity.py
allfiles.summary: Dataset loader for RealToxicityPrompts: loads and shuffles the HF dataset, safely coerces prompt toxicity scores to floats, and emits ToxicitySample records with challenge metadata for the legacy toxicity evaluation.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Lazy-import barrel for the legacy evaluators (toxicity/bias/factuality) so heavyweight ML deps load only when used; not part of the default quantization pipeline.

allfiles.name: bias_eval.py
allfiles.summary: Legacy evaluator for social-bias multiple-choice QA: parses letter/digit answers from model responses, scores accuracy and stereotyped-answer rates, and summarizes results with per-axis breakdowns.

allfiles.name: factuality_eval.py
allfiles.summary: Legacy evaluator for TruthfulQA-style multiple-choice factuality: parses answer letters/digits from responses, scores correctness against gold indices, and optionally rates open-ended answers with an LLM judge.

allfiles.name: toxicity_eval.py
allfiles.summary: Legacy toxicity evaluator with pluggable scorer backends (Detoxify or a HuggingFace text-classification pipeline): scores generated continuations for toxicity probability, thresholds them, and summarizes toxicity rate with bootstrap confidence intervals.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the agent-harness API: check runners, status/start-packet builders, and handoff/dashboard/checklist renderers.

allfiles.name: agent.py
allfiles.summary: Core agent-harness engine: loads the machine-readable policy (configs/artifact_policy.yaml), verifies immutable TC1 artifacts via SHA-256 manifests, enforces CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md sync and PROJECT_LOG.md discipline, runs stale-text/redaction scans and report-freshness checks, and renders agent status, start packets, HANDOFF.md, the agent dashboard, and the TC1 checklist.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the judge-validation API (backends, run_judge_validation, parsing helpers).

allfiles.name: validation.py
allfiles.summary: Judge-model validation layer (D16): JudgeBackend interface with HarmBench-Llama-2-13b-cls (primary ASR scorer), LlamaGuard, and OpenAI-compatible API backends; run_judge_validation reads raw.jsonl HarmBench outputs, classifies each response, and writes redacted scores.judge/summary.judge sidecars (IDs + booleans only) without ever mutating raw artifacts.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the metrics I/O and bootstrap-CI helpers from aggregate.py for use across the pipeline and analysis layers.

allfiles.name: aggregate.py
allfiles.summary: Core metrics I/O utilities: JSONL append/read (with gzip support), JSON writing, summary CSV upsert, nested-dict flattening for CSV, radar CSV export, and a bootstrap confidence-interval estimator used by benchmark aggregation.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Lazy-import barrel exposing the model layer (HFModelLoader, TextGenerator, ModelSpec) without importing torch at package import time.

allfiles.name: generation.py
allfiles.summary: Batched text generation over loaded HF models: DecodingConfig (greedy, temperature 0.0 defaults), TextGenerator that applies the native chat template with enable_thinking=False and records prompt provenance, plus global seeding for determinism.

allfiles.name: loader.py
allfiles.summary: HF model + tokenizer loading with dtype/device resolution and on-the-fly quantization: builds BitsAndBytesConfig for NF4 4-bit or LLM.int8, verifies quantization actually engaged after load (hard fail otherwise), and defines the ModelSpec contract.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Package barrel exposing the single-run (run_quant_benchmark) and matrix (run_quant_matrix) orchestration modules of the quantization pipeline.

allfiles.name: run_benchmark.py
allfiles.summary: CLI orchestrator for the legacy ethical benchmark: parses arguments, validates the YAML config, loads each model, streams dataset samples through batched generation and the task-specific evaluator, and persists raw JSONL, summary JSON/CSV, and radar-chart CSV outputs with resume support.

allfiles.name: run_quant_benchmark.py
allfiles.summary: Single-run orchestrator: loads a (possibly NF4/INT8-quantized) HF model per the validated config, batches benchmark prompts through the generator, scores each response via the benchmark plugin, and persists raw.jsonl, summary.json, and summary CSV artifacts with resume support based on processed prompt IDs.

allfiles.name: run_quant_matrix.py
allfiles.summary: Matrix orchestrator that runs every configured model across its benchmarks; in reuse mode it loads each model once, runs all its benchmarks sequentially via execute_quant_benchmark_loaded, and releases GPU memory (gc + cuda.empty_cache) in a try/finally between models.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Package barrel for the quant module re-exporting QuantizationConfig and load_quant_config from config_schema.

allfiles.name: config_schema.py
allfiles.summary: Pydantic schema layer for the whole study: defines decoding, model-entry, benchmark, and SLURM config models plus cross-validation (every pair_id needs a baseline and a quantized member; model benchmark lists must reference declared benchmarks) and the load_quant_config YAML loader.

allfiles.name: fyp_cli.py
allfiles.summary: Unified project CLI with subcommands for smoke runs, single benchmark runs, the full model x benchmark matrix, pairwise analysis, SLURM cluster generate/submit/check, and agent-harness status/start packets, dispatching into the pipeline, analysis, cluster, and harness modules.

allfiles.name: generate_slurm_jobs.py
allfiles.summary: Root-level convenience entrypoint that simply invokes the SLURM job generator's main() from ethical_benchmark.cluster.generate_jobs.

allfiles.name: package.json
allfiles.summary: Node manifest pinning docx-js for the report/thesis builder scripts under scripts/.

allfiles.name: pyproject.toml
allfiles.summary: Python project metadata and tooling configuration for the ethical_benchmark package.

allfiles.name: requirements-dashboard.txt
allfiles.summary: Extra Python dependencies for the Streamlit dashboard (streamlit, plotly).

allfiles.name: requirements.txt
allfiles.summary: Pinned Python dependencies: transformers/torch/bitsandbytes stack, judge tokenizer deps (sentencepiece/protobuf/tiktoken), optional OpenAI second-judge client, and pytest.

allfiles.name: run_benchmark.py
allfiles.summary: Thin repo-root launcher that delegates to the pipeline's single-run benchmark entry point for backwards-compatible invocation.

allfiles.name: run_quant_benchmark.py
allfiles.summary: Repo-root shim that invokes ethical_benchmark.pipeline.run_quant_benchmark.main so a single quantized benchmark run can be launched directly (e.g. from sbatch scripts).

allfiles.name: run_quant_matrix.py
allfiles.summary: Repo-root shim that invokes ethical_benchmark.pipeline.run_quant_matrix.main to launch the full model-by-benchmark matrix (used by SLURM matrix jobs).

allfiles.name: agent_check.py
allfiles.summary: CLI entry for `make agent-check`: runs the full harness gate (docs sync, log discipline, artifacts, stale text, pytest) and prints pass/fail results.

allfiles.name: build_agent_harness_architecture_doc.py
allfiles.summary: python-docx builder for the agent-harness architecture Word document: styled tables, callouts, two-column cards, and scenario walkthroughs.

allfiles.name: build_agentic_report.js
allfiles.summary: docx-js builder for the agentic-workflow meetup report, with inline SVG figures (ladder, loop, harness, memory, tests) rasterized to PNG.

allfiles.name: build_fyp_report.js
allfiles.summary: Archived first-generation FYP report builder (superseded; kept for provenance — v5 is canonical).

allfiles.name: build_fyp_report_v2.js
allfiles.summary: Archived FYP report builder v2 (superseded by v5).

allfiles.name: build_fyp_report_v3.js
allfiles.summary: Archived FYP report builder v3 (superseded by v5).

allfiles.name: build_fyp_report_v4.js
allfiles.summary: Archived FYP report builder v4 (superseded by v5 in the D41 512-primary re-base).

allfiles.name: build_fyp_report_v5.js
allfiles.summary: CANONICAL FYP report builder (D41, 512-primary): docx-js source generating docs/FYP_Report_2026-07-01_v5.docx via `make report`. The script is the source of truth; the docx is the artifact and is never hand-edited.

allfiles.name: build_fyp_thesis.js
allfiles.summary: Archived first thesis builder (superseded by v4; archived docx carries a banner).

allfiles.name: build_fyp_thesis_v2.js
allfiles.summary: Archived thesis builder v2 (superseded by v4).

allfiles.name: build_fyp_thesis_v3.js
allfiles.summary: Archived thesis builder v3 (superseded by v4).

allfiles.name: build_fyp_thesis_v4.js
allfiles.summary: CANONICAL thesis builder (D43 512 mirror): generates docs/FYP_Thesis_2026-07-02_v4.docx via `make thesis`, claim-locked and adversarially verified.

allfiles.name: capture_refusal_margin.py
allfiles.summary: TC1 capture script that re-runs HarmBench prompts through each model recording first-token logits and refusal-margin diagnostics as redacted sidecars for the mechanistic refusal analysis.

allfiles.name: generate_agent_dashboard.py
allfiles.summary: Thin CLI wrapper regenerating docs/AGENT_DASHBOARD.md from harness state (make agent-dashboard).

allfiles.name: generate_agent_harness_architecture_diagram.py
allfiles.summary: Generates the agent-harness architecture diagram as SVG and editable draw.io XML (cards, pills, edge routing).

allfiles.name: generate_architecture_diagram.py
allfiles.summary: Generates the system architecture diagram (pipeline, benchmarks, analysis layers) as SVG and draw.io XML for the report.

allfiles.name: generate_handoff.py
allfiles.summary: Thin CLI wrapper regenerating docs/HANDOFF.md from live repo state (make agent-handoff).

allfiles.name: generate_integrated_architecture_diagram.py
allfiles.summary: Generates the integrated architecture diagram combining the benchmark pipeline with the agent-harness contract band, folder tree, and skills, as SVG and draw.io XML.

allfiles.name: generate_repo_hierarchy_diagram.py
allfiles.summary: Generates the repository hierarchy diagram (SVG + draw.io) used in report/docs.

allfiles.name: generate_sensitivity_jobs.py
allfiles.summary: Generates decoding-sensitivity sbatch jobs (temperature/multi-seed sweep) plus the matching judge-scoring sbatch for TC1.

allfiles.name: generate_tc1_checklist.py
allfiles.summary: Thin CLI wrapper regenerating docs/TC1_AGENT_CHECKLIST.md (make agent-tc1-checklist).

allfiles.name: genlen_512_analysis.py
allfiles.summary: Generation-length study comparing the 128- vs 512-token budgets: per-pair kappa, McNemar, BH-FDR, and the prefix-truncation analysis that established the 128-era Qwen +0.055 ASR increase as a truncation artefact (report section 6.16).

allfiles.name: harmbench_category_breakdown.py
allfiles.summary: Per-category HarmBench ASR breakdown (judge-scored) across pairs, showing which harm categories drive deltas.

allfiles.name: harness_eval.py
allfiles.summary: Named wrapper (make harness-eval) running the focused harness self-evaluation test suite.

allfiles.name: human_label_audit.py
allfiles.summary: Human annotation audit tooling (T30): draws a stratified candidate sample into a labeling sheet (CSV/HTML), then scores human labels against the judge and v2 regex with kappa and precision/recall/F1, and applies adjudicated labels.

allfiles.name: judge_agreement.py
allfiles.summary: GPU-free comparison of the primary HarmBench classifier judge against the v2 regex scorer: per-model agreement %, Cohen's kappa and 2x2 confusion, per-pair delta-ASR under both scorers, and whether the interpretation label would flip under the judge. Writes results/analysis/judge_agreement.{json,csv} from redacted (IDs + booleans) sidecars only.

allfiles.name: judge_pairwise_agreement.py
allfiles.summary: Judge-vs-judge robustness check (T22/W3): cross-checks the primary HarmBench classifier against a second independent judge (GPT-4o api_judge) on agreement, kappa, and per-pair delta-ASR sign/significance concordance, reusing the same McNemar/bootstrap apparatus as the primary analysis. Writes results/analysis/judge_pairwise_agreement.{json,csv}.

allfiles.name: make_figures.py
allfiles.summary: Matplotlib figure generation for the report: capability anchor, ASR forest plot, precision sweep, judge-vs-proxy comparison, category ASR, and multi-seed sensitivity figures.

allfiles.name: mmlu_subject_breakdown.py
allfiles.summary: Per-subject MMLU accuracy breakdown across pairs to localize capability changes under quantization.

allfiles.name: multiple_comparisons.py
allfiles.summary: Statistical-rigour layer for the pairwise study: McNemar exact tests on paired judge outcomes, paired bootstrap CIs, Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction across all pair-by-benchmark contrasts, plus MDE/power notes; writes analysis/multiple_comparisons.json consumed by the dashboard and report.

allfiles.name: number_bible_512.py
allfiles.summary: Prints the authoritative 'number bible' of 512-primary values from results_512/analysis so every number inserted into the report/thesis can be cross-checked against one source.

allfiles.name: precision_sweep_analysis.py
allfiles.summary: Builds the fp16 -> INT8 -> NF4 precision-sweep table per pair and benchmark: metric trend classification (graded/cliff/non-monotonic) and INT8 McNemar significance; feeds the report's precision-point section.

allfiles.name: prefetch_tc1.py
allfiles.summary: Head-node pre-cache script: downloads datasets, model weights, and optionally the 13B judge into the HF cache so offline compute nodes (HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1) can run.

allfiles.name: refusal_margin_analysis.py
allfiles.summary: Analyzes captured refusal-margin sidecars: per-pair paired margin shifts, AUC of margin vs judge outcome, bootstrap CIs — the mechanistic complement to behavioural ASR deltas.

allfiles.name: rescore_harmbench.py
allfiles.summary: Post-hoc rescoring script that re-applies the corrected v2 refusal classifier to existing HarmBench and XSTest raw.jsonl results and writes derived scores.v2.jsonl / summary.v2.json sidecars plus redacted diagnostics, without touching the immutable TC1-original artifacts or re-running inference.

allfiles.name: run_judge_validation.py
allfiles.summary: CLI for the judge validation pipeline (TC1 sbatch entry): selects backend/precision/aliases and invokes run_judge_validation per model.

allfiles.name: sensitivity_analysis.py
allfiles.summary: Decoding-sensitivity analysis: compares greedy vs sampled-decoding deltas (v2 and judge ASR) to check robustness of the headline conclusions.

allfiles.name: verify_report_claims.py
allfiles.summary: MACHINE CLAIM LOCK (make verify-claims): ~55 checks pinning report and thesis numbers plus IEEE citation consistency to the primary artifacts in results_512/analysis; rides pytest via tests/test_report_claims.py so stale claims fail CI.

allfiles.name: submit_slurm_jobs.py
allfiles.summary: Root-level convenience entrypoint that simply invokes the SLURM submission helper's main() from ethical_benchmark.cluster.submit_jobs.

allfiles.name: __init__.py
allfiles.summary: Package marker for the test suite.

allfiles.name: conftest.py
allfiles.summary: Shared pytest fixtures: canned ToxicitySample/BiasSample/FactualitySample lists, a default legacy benchmark config dict, and a quantization-study config dict used across the test suite.

allfiles.name: test_agent_harness.py
allfiles.summary: Harness self-evaluation suite: proves the gate catches doc drift, missing PROJECT_LOG updates, stale-text/kappa regressions, redaction leaks, immutable-artifact mutation, and report-freshness violations on fixture repos.

allfiles.name: test_arc.py
allfiles.summary: Unit tests for the ARC-Challenge plugin: answer-key resolution, dataset-schema parsing into BenchmarkItems, exact-match scoring, accuracy aggregation, and registry construction.

allfiles.name: test_dashboard_data.py
allfiles.summary: Tests for the dashboard data layer: loader resilience, judge-primary interpretations matching committed headline artifacts, v2-proxy divergence, config-generation round-trips, and protected-results-dir guards.

allfiles.name: test_dashboard_theme.py
allfiles.summary: Tests for the dashboard theme: CSS token presence, hero/stat-tile/badge HTML structure, and judge-primary stat-line rendering in pair cards.

allfiles.name: test_datasets.py
allfiles.summary: Unit tests for the legacy dataset loaders: sample dataclass creation/immutability, choice and gold-index parsing helpers, MC target extraction, and loader input validation errors.

allfiles.name: test_evaluators.py
allfiles.summary: Unit tests for the legacy evaluators: answer-index parsing for bias and factuality, toxicity probability extraction from classifier outputs, and end-to-end evaluate/summarize behavior of BiasEvaluator and FactualityEvaluator.

allfiles.name: test_int8_scoping.py
allfiles.summary: Guards INT8 scoping: judge agreement, pairwise, and breakdown scripts must not reopen committed old-6 judge sidecars when scoring INT8 members (zero-drift rule).

allfiles.name: test_judge_agreement.py
allfiles.summary: Tests kappa computation in judge_agreement: known values, perfect agreement, total disagreement, degenerate and empty cases.

allfiles.name: test_judge_pairwise.py
allfiles.summary: Tests for the API second-judge path and agreement math: yes/no mapping, model-id exposure, kappa values, and null-outcome skipping.

allfiles.name: test_judge_validation.py
allfiles.summary: Judge-layer tests with a stub backend: official prompt fidelity, redaction enforcement, sidecar writing/idempotency, ASR math, error counting, and precision/VRAM helpers.

allfiles.name: test_matrix_reuse.py
allfiles.summary: Tests the matrix runner's model-reuse logic with monkeypatched dummy loader/generator stacks: reuse mode must load each model exactly once across its benchmarks, while no-reuse mode must delegate to the single-run entry per (model, benchmark).

allfiles.name: test_metrics_and_config.py
allfiles.summary: Unit tests for the metrics I/O layer (bootstrap CI, CSV flattening, JSONL round-trips, JSON writing, summary-CSV upsert, radar CSV) plus config validation failure modes (missing models, invalid dtype, negative token counts, out-of-range temperature).

allfiles.name: test_models.py
allfiles.summary: Unit tests for the model layer using fake torch/transformers doubles: ModelSpec immutability, device/dtype resolution, chat-template thinking-flag handling, prompt provenance, quantization-active detection, and attn_implementation propagation.

allfiles.name: test_pipeline.py
allfiles.summary: Unit tests for legacy pipeline helper functions: batching, output-path construction with alias sanitization, and radar-dimension building with clamping.

allfiles.name: test_precision_sweep.py
allfiles.summary: Tests for precision_sweep_analysis: trend classification, graceful handling of pending INT8 runs, v2-sidecar preference, and reproduction of the documented Llama INT8 McNemar result.

allfiles.name: test_quant_analysis.py
allfiles.summary: Unit tests for the pairwise delta/interpretation engine: classify_pair_change labels, two-layer evidence status, McNemar and bootstrap helpers, v2-sidecar preference, and NF4/INT8 member selection guards.

allfiles.name: test_quant_config_schema.py
allfiles.summary: Tests locking down the live config shape (10 models, 5 pairs, 4 families; Phi eager attention, Mistral defaults, shared methodology across new pairs) and the ModelEntry attn_implementation validator.

allfiles.name: test_quant_int8.py
allfiles.summary: Tests for the INT8 precision point: quant_method schema validation, ModelSpec propagation, 8-bit detection, quantization-engaged guard, loader branch selection (int8 vs nf4), and the tc1_int8 config contents.

allfiles.name: test_quant_pipeline_utils.py
allfiles.summary: Unit tests for the single-run pipeline's helper functions: record schema validation (accept/reject), resume filtering via prepare_remaining_items, and processed-prompt-ID recovery from raw.jsonl.

allfiles.name: test_quant_smoke.py
allfiles.summary: End-to-end smoke tests of the single-run pipeline using dummy model/plugin doubles: each benchmark type runs through execute_quant_benchmark producing raw.jsonl and summary.json, and identical seeds yield identical prompt ordering across runs.

allfiles.name: test_refusal_margin.py
allfiles.summary: Math-level tests for refusal-margin diagnostics: sign/symmetry/shift-invariance of the margin, entropy and top1-top2 gap behaviour, and token-set construction edge cases.

allfiles.name: test_refusal_parser.py
allfiles.summary: Unit tests for the shared v2 scoring utilities: refusal-pattern positives/negatives (including curly-apostrophe normalisation), malformed-response detection, and the choice-index parser's priority cascade and edge cases.

allfiles.name: test_report_claims.py
allfiles.summary: Pytest bridge that runs the machine claim lock (verify_report_claims) so report/thesis number drift fails the test suite.

allfiles.name: test_sensitivity.py
allfiles.summary: Tests for the decoding-sensitivity study tooling: validates the sensitivity config is stochastic and schema-valid, checks delta-summary stability math, and verifies the sensitivity sbatch generator and v2-summary-preferring ASR reader loaded dynamically from scripts/.

allfiles.name: test_slurm_helpers.py
allfiles.summary: Tests for SLURM job generation and submission: job counts per grouping mode, matrix-runner reuse under group_by=model, dry-run submission with submitted_jobs.json output, work_dir/setup_commands/log-dir prologue content, relative script resolution, and walltime format validation.

allfiles.name: test_xstest_benign.py
allfiles.summary: Unit tests for XSTest benign-prompt classification: contrast types are unsafe, plain categories are safe by convention, explicit labels and id cues are honoured, and unrecognised rows default to benign.
